title: Coding Style
documentation:
## Purpose

Coding Style describes the formatting conventions, naming rules, and stylistic preferences that generated or reviewed code should follow. It is a free-form text field, allowing project-specific style guides to be expressed in plain language.

## When to use

Use this media type when a code generation node should adhere to a particular house style, linter configuration, or team convention. It works alongside [Programming Language](https://capdag.com/browse/media:programming-language;textable) and [Code Context](https://capdag.com/browse/media:code-contextable).

## Notes

Keep style descriptions concise and actionable. Examples such as "use snake_case for variables", "prefer early returns", or "max line length 100" are more effective than vague directives.
